en: One was the first thing I wrote on the... This is the first thing I wrote on the internet, did very well.

zh: 是的，所以 Google 用他们的「它忙吗」来做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, all these other ways that you could solve this problem.
zh: 是的，所有这些你可以解决这个问题的其他方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was very cool.
zh: 那非常酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was amazing.
zh: 那太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nobody needs it, really.
zh: 其实没人需要它。

speaker: Unknown
en: If there's still things you want to ask-
zh: 如果你还有想问的事情——

speaker: Unknown
en: You need once in a while.
zh: 你偶尔需要一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need once a quarter and it's holy shit, that's so good.
zh: 你一个季度需要一次，那感觉就是天哪，太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you can't make a business out of that, so I'm glad that we exited.
zh: 但你没法以此做成一门生意，所以我很庆幸我们退出了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today's episode is brought to you by DX, the developer intelligence platform designed by leading researchers.
zh: 今天的节目由 DX 带给大家，DX 是一个由顶尖研究人员设计的开发者智能平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: To thrive in the AI era, organizations need to adapt quickly, but many organization leaders struggle to answer pressing questions, like which tools are working?
zh: 要在 AI 时代蓬勃发展，组织需要快速适应，但许多组织领导者都在努力回答紧迫的问题，比如哪些工具在起作用？

speaker: Unknown
en: How are they being used?
zh: 它们是如何被使用的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's actually driving value?
zh: 什么真正在驱动价值？

speaker: Unknown
en: DX provides the data and insights that leaders need to navigate this shift.
zh: DX 提供领导者驾驭这一转变所需的数据和洞察。

speaker: Unknown
en: With DX, companies like Dropbox, booking.com, Adyen, and Intercom get a deep understanding of how AI is providing value to their developers and what impact AI is having on engineering productivity.
zh: 有了 DX，像 Dropbox、booking.com、Adyen 和 Intercom 这样的公司能深入了解 AI 如何为他们的开发者提供价值，以及 AI 对工程生产力有什么影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: To learn more, visit DX's website at getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 要了解更多，请访问 DX 的网站 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 就是 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so you're mentioning the atheist bot with bunch of religious dating sites.
zh: 好的，所以你提到了那个无神论机器人配对了一堆宗教约会网站。

speaker: Unknown
en: Reminded me that you were my first online date ever.
zh: 这让我想起你曾是我第一次在线约会的对象。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hell yeah.
zh: 嘿，是啊。

speaker: Unknown
en: Snatched right up.
zh: 立马就抢到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Platform called HowAboutWe?
zh: 叫 HowAboutWe 的平台？

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, you had had other dates.
zh: 我是说，你之前有过其他约会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where's this going?
zh: 这话要往哪带？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyway, no.
zh: 无论如何，不。

speaker: Unknown
en: See, I think you had a thing for designers, and I wonder if you regret that preference.
zh: 瞧，我觉得你对设计师情有独钟，我想知道你是否后悔那个偏好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Designer artists-
zh: 设计师、艺术家——

speaker: Unknown
en: No, not at all.
zh: 不，一点也不。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... stereotype.
zh: ……刻板印象。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, no.
zh: 不，不。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not.
zh: 我没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're pretty neat.
zh: 你很整洁。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah.
zh: 是的，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's true.
zh: 是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're chaotic.
zh: 你很混乱。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is, we could talk about your process, but it's a messy process.
zh: 也就是，我们可以聊聊你的流程，但那是个很乱的流程。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's true.
zh: 是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have a good yin/yang.
zh: 我们有很好的阴阳互补。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I'm just like you're unstressed, unbothered.
zh: 是的，我只是觉得你很从容，不受干扰。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm bothered.
zh: 我会受困扰。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm stressed.
zh: 我会感到压力。

speaker: Lenny Rachitsky
en: You stress things everywhere. We get cleaners. They help things out. Yeah, it's true.
zh: 你到处都给人压力。我们请了清洁工。他们帮忙搞定事情。是的，是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know.
zh: 我知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Privilege.
zh: 特权。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's worth it.
zh: 这是值得的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so you're okay with designers still.
zh: 好的，所以你依然喜欢设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: Good.
zh: 很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then-
zh: 然后——

speaker: Unknown
en: As a wife.
zh: 作为一个妻子。

speaker: Michelle Rial
en: Yeah, right, right.
zh: 是的，对，对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Highly recommend.
zh: 强烈推荐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Lenny Rachitsky
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, great.
zh: 哦，太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I was going to ask, do you have any tips for... Because there's something you said recently, which is you don't have any coworkers.
zh: 是的，我正想问，你有什么建议吗……因为你最近说了些什么，就是你没有任何同事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "I'm your coworker.
zh: 我当时说，“我是你的同事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We work at home."
zh: 我们在家工作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, do you ever feel lonely because you were always really social?
zh: 是的，你会觉得孤单吗，因为你以前真的非常善于社交？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then this goes into too even before you were niche famous, or whatever we want to call it-
zh: 这甚至可以追溯到在你成为小众名人之前，或者不管我们想怎么称呼它——

speaker: Lenny Rachitsky
en: Yeah, that's how I describe it.
zh: 是的，这就是我对它的描述。

speaker: Unknown
en: People were always coming up to you on the street like, "Lenny."
zh: 人们过去总是在街上走向你，“Lenny。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, from Airbnb.
zh: 是的，来自 Airbnb。

speaker: Unknown
en: From Airbnb because you're organizing things.
zh: 来自 Airbnb，因为你组织事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you feel like you miss office culture?
zh: 你觉得你会怀念办公室文化吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: People ask me that over the years and I've always like, "No, it's amazing.
zh: 多年来人们总是问我这个问题，我总是说，“不，这很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't need that.
zh: 我不需要那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just do my own thing and I don't need people around."
zh: 我只做我自己的事情，我不需要周围有人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I've started to feel that a little bit of just like, huh, there's no one.
zh: 但我开始感觉到一点点，就是，嗯，这里没有人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just sit at home all day and just like...
zh: 我只是整天坐在家里，就像……

speaker: Unknown
en: Our dog likes to be right next to you.
zh: 我们的狗喜欢紧挨着你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, he's sweet.
zh: 是的，他很甜。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do feel like I do miss it now.
zh: 我确实觉得我现在确实怀念它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not like a huge problem, but it's just like it'd be cool to just jam with someone on stuff.
zh: 这不是一个大问题，但就像，能和别人一起钻研一些事情会很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a team and I have you and Twitter.
zh: 我有一个团队，我有你，还有 Twitter。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, you like to go to coffee shops.
zh: 嗯，你喜欢去咖啡馆。

speaker: Lenny Rachitsky
en: I like to go to coffee.
zh: 我喜欢去喝咖啡。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you get?
zh: 你点什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's like bowling alone.
zh: 但这就像单人保龄球。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if that's the good metaphor.
zh: 我不知道那是不是个好比喻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have your headphones.
zh: 而且你有你的耳机。

speaker: Unknown
en: You put your headphones, too.
zh: 你戴上你的耳机，也戴。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my headphones on, yeah.
zh: 而且我的耳机戴着，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're focused.
zh: 而且你很专注。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so it would be fun.
zh: 是的，所以那样会很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have an awesome team and we jam on stuff, but it's not like... I think I do miss having someone next to me that I'm just working with on the same thing.
zh: 我有一个很棒的团队，我们一起钻研事情，但这不像……我想我确实怀念身边有个人，只是和我一起做同样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I also am trying very hard to never have full-time employees.
zh: 但我也非常努力地不想拥有全职员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm trying to keep things really simple because it's so easy to build this thing into this whole thing that's complicated that I'm like, "What have I done?"
zh: 我试图让事情保持非常简单，因为很容易把这件事构建成这种复杂的整体，我就像，“我做了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the challenges with this life is you can create a job for yourself that you hate by doing things that people want you to do or by following opportunities that feel big and then you're like, "I hate this."
zh: 这种生活的一个挑战是，你可以为自己创造一份你讨厌的工作，通过做人们希望你做的事情，或者通过追随那些感觉很重大的机会，然后你就想，“我讨厌这个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm trying to be really careful about what I commit to and do.
zh: 我正试着对我承诺的事情和做的事情非常小心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Coming back to your question, I do miss it some.
zh: 回到你的问题，我确实有些怀念它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's like a new thing I've realized that I miss is being around other people, like working on the same thing.
zh: 是的，就像我新意识到的一件事是我怀念的是，和其他人在一起，比如做同样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so speaking of other people coming up to you and being around other people, I think it's become harder for you now that people you don't know come up to you because I think you actually have a little bit of face blindness.
zh: 是的，说到陌生人走向你和身处其他人中间，我觉得这对你来说变得更难了，因为你不认识的人走向你，因为我觉得你其实有一点脸盲症。

speaker: Unknown
en: People who do know you come up to you and you're waiting to know if they just listen to your podcast or if you-
zh: 认识你的人走向你，你在等着搞清楚他们只是听了你的播客，还是你——

speaker: Unknown
en: I know if I know them.
zh: 我知道我是否认识他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is a big problem for me.
zh: 是的，这对我来说是个大问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like another brain disorder, where I just don't remember people's names.
zh: 这就像另一种大脑障碍，我就是记不住别人的名字。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm so bad at it.
zh: 我在这方面太差了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just like, "I know you.
zh: 我只是说，“我认识你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who are you?"
zh: 你是谁？”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like people texting you from a new number, "I'm like, who is this?"
zh: 这就像人们用新号码给你发短信，“我就想问，这是谁？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, sometimes I feel like the Devil Wears Prada assistant where I'm like, "That's Emily.
zh: 是的，有时我觉得自己像《穿普拉达的女王》里的助理，我会说，“那是 Emily。

speaker: Unknown
en: You worked with her on Airbnb," or that's-
zh: 你在 Airbnb 和她共事过的，”或者那是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Wait, you don't do that.
zh: 等等，你不会那么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need to do that.
zh: 你得那么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do it sometimes.
zh: 我有时候会那么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: You do that sometimes?
zh: 你有时候会那么做？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so bad at that.
zh: 是的，我太不擅长那个了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so if you come up to me and you say hello and I don't recognize you, please, I'm sorry.
zh: 好吧，所以如果你走过来跟我打招呼，而我没认出你，拜托，我很抱歉。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm really bad at it.
zh: 我真的很不擅长这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's gotten worse because now I know more people through the podcast and the newsletter and like-
zh: 是的，情况变得更糟了，因为现在通过播客和通讯我认识了更多人，就像——

speaker: Unknown
en: Right, and you just know a lot of people.
zh: 对，而且你认识很多人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's like a lot of faces.
zh: 是的，有很多面孔。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're very kind with people like you.
zh: 你对人非常友善，就像你一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I try, I try, I try.
zh: 我努力，我努力，我努力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so going back to the unbothered vibe that you have, tell me about a time you've been really stressed in your business and then a time you've been really stressed in your personal life.
zh: 好吧，回到你那种不受打扰的氛围，告诉我一次你在生意上真的很有压力的时候，还有一次你在个人生活中真的很有压力的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so on the business side, so I have this product pass.
zh: 好吧，所以在商业方面，我有这个产品通行证。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a subscriber to my newsletter, you get 23 incredible products.
zh: 作为我通讯的订阅者，你会获得 23 个不可思议的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I used to read them all: Linear, Mobb and Lovable, Replit, Gamma.
zh: 我以前会把它们都读完：Linear、Mobb 和 Lovable、Replit、Gamma。

speaker: Unknown
en: I stopped doing this because there's too many now I had that end of my podcast.
zh: 我不再做这个了，因为现在太多了，我在我播客的最后那期。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just read through all, and now I can't do it anymore.
zh: 我只是通读了所有内容，现在我再也做不到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's too many.
zh: 实在太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I had a launch about a year ago, it's about a year actually where I launched... You get a free year of Cursor and Lovable and Bolt and Replit and v0.
zh: 我大约一年前搞过一次发布，实际上大约一年前，我发布了……你会得到 Cursor、Lovable、Bolt、Replit 和 v0 的一年免费使用权。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's five.
zh: 我想那是五个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that was way too good an offer.
zh: 所以那是一个太好的优惠。

speaker: Unknown
en: People came for it, bad people.
zh: 人们为了它而来，坏人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just had so many fraudsters, mostly in China it turned out.
zh: 我只是遇到了那么多诈骗犯，结果大部分在中国。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like all these fraud rings in China trying to find ways to steal all these free goodies.
zh: 就像中国所有的这些诈骗团伙试图想办法偷走所有这些免费赠品。

speaker: Unknown
en: They set up all these crazy attacks, and I had to work with Stripe and Substack to just shut them down.
zh: 他们设置了所有这些疯狂的攻击，我不得不与 Stripe 和 Substack 合作来关闭它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: They just found all these little exploits in our API that we built.
zh: 他们只是在我们构建的 API 中发现了所有这些小漏洞。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was such a nightmare because we're just waking up to like every night.
zh: 这简直是噩梦，因为我们只是每晚醒来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was hard to sleep.
zh: 很难入睡。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have this engineer, Este, who's so incredible.
zh: 我有一位工程师，Este，他非常了不起。

speaker: Unknown
en: He didn't sleep for a week stopping, filling all these holes because there's a lot of smart, clever, bad guys out there.
zh: 他一周没睡，堵住了所有这些漏洞，因为外面有很多聪明、狡猾的坏人。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was extremely stressful.
zh: 那压力非常大。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could trickle down into the whole thing falling apart if everyone's like, "Oh my God, can't trust Lenny anymore."
zh: 这可能会蔓延导致整个事情分崩离析，如果大家都说，“天哪，不能再相信 Lenny 了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: As you remember, it was very stressful.
zh: 如你所记得的，那非常有压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't sleep too well, and it was very scary.
zh: 我睡得不太好，那很可怕。

speaker: Unknown
en: They just kept popping up, and there's all these rings.
zh: 他们只是不断冒出来，而且有所有这些团伙。

speaker: Unknown
en: It went viral in China, too.
zh: 它在中国也病毒式传播了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's like in the student network, they just, "Holy shit, you get a year free of Cursor and Lovable and Replit and Bolt and v0?"
zh: 就像在学生网络里，他们只是，“天哪，你可以免费使用一年 Cursor、Lovable、Replit、Bolt 和 v0？”

speaker: Unknown
en: It was very stressful.
zh: 那非常有压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: To answer your question on the personal side, okay, so when our baby was born, the birth was very complicated.
zh: 为了回答你在个人方面的问题，好吧，所以当我们宝宝出生时，生产过程非常复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: You had to get a C-section because the induction wasn't getting it there because it was-
zh: 你不得不做剖腹产，因为引产没起作用，因为那是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Complicated.
zh: 复杂的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's complicated.
zh: 很复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: We won't get into it.
zh: 我们不谈这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Normally I feel like I would tell this story, but-
zh: 通常我觉得我会讲这个故事，但是——

speaker: Unknown
en: You weren't awake.
zh: 你当时是醒着的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But this is also like when Harry Met Sally, but it's like how the pregnant person almost died.
zh: 但这也就像《当哈利遇到莎莉》一样，但就像是那个孕妇差点死掉了一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's from your perspective because I didn't get to experience it.
zh: 但这是从你的角度出发，因为我没有经历到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so what happened is you had to do a cesarean, and so the idea is they bring you into the operating room, they do the anesthetic, the epidural or whatever it's called, and then they let the husband and the partner in.
zh: 是的，所以发生的事情是你必须做剖腹产，他们的想法是把你带进手术室，他们做麻醉，硬膜外麻醉或者不管它叫什么，然后他们让丈夫和伴侣进去。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I was waiting in the hall waiting for them to do the anesthetic.
zh: 所以我当时在走廊里等他们做麻醉。

speaker: Unknown
en: With your scrubs.
zh: 穿着你的洗手衣。

speaker: Unknown
en: They gave me a whole bunny suit.
zh: 他们给了我一整套兔子服。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, super sterile, just standing there in this bunny suit thing.
zh: 是的，超级无菌，只是穿着那套兔子服站在那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: The idea is they do the anesthetic, and I can come in and then watch the birth and all that stuff.
zh: 想法是他们做完麻醉，我就可以进来然后观看出生和所有那些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Five, 10 minutes in, I just hear beep, beep, beep, beep, beep, beep, beep, beep, beep, beep, beep.
zh: 进去了五、十分钟后，我就听到哔、哔、哔、哔、哔、哔、哔、哔、哔、哔、哔。

speaker: Unknown
en: The doctor runs out, scrubs her hands, runs back in.
zh: 医生跑了出来，洗手，又跑了回去。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then two other people run from another room down the hall into the room.
zh: 然后另外两个人从走廊另一头的另一个房间跑进了那个房间。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Uh-oh."
zh: 他们像是说，“糟糕。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like-
zh: 我像是——

speaker: Michelle Rial
en: You were scared?
zh: 你害怕了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I was very scared because I had no idea what was going on.
zh: 我非常害怕，因为我不知道发生了什么事。

speaker: Unknown
en: That didn't sound good.
zh: 那听起来不太好。

speaker: Unknown
